edges.description: Loss of CBL-mediated ubiquitination and degradation
Sustained HGF-MET signaling promotes tumor growth

nodes.description: MET signaling promotes cell proliferation, survival, motility, and invasion. The HGF-MET axis is particularly important for epithelial-mesenchymal transition and metastasis.

MET exon 14 skipping results from splice site mutations that cause exclusion of exon 14 during pre-mRNA splicing. Exon 14 encodes a juxtamembrane regulatory domain containing a CBL E3 ubiquitin ligase binding site (Y1003). Loss of this domain prevents receptor ubiquitination and degradation, leading to prolonged MET signaling after ligand stimulation.

biological_processes: transmembrane receptor protein tyrosine kinase signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: MET Exon 14 Mutations in Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer Are Associated With Advanced Age and Stage-Dependent MET Genomic Amplification and c-Met Overexpression.
evidence.snippet: MET exon 14 mutations were identified in 28 of 933 nonsquamous NSCLCs (3.0%) and were not seen in other cancer types in this study.

nodes.description: METex14 skipping results in prolonged receptor activation and downstream signaling through RAS-MAPK, PI3K-AKT, and STAT3 pathways. HGF (hepatocyte growth factor) ligand binding triggers signaling, but receptor downregulation is impaired.
